a260717p1.summary: Earlier this week, Google DeepMind Chief Executive Officer Demis Hassabis unveiled a proposal for a new international watchdog …
a260717p1.title: Source: Demis Hassabis plans to hold meetings with US policymakers in Washington next week about his proposed US-based Standards Body for "Frontier-class" AI (Shirin Ghaffary/Bloomberg)

a260717p10.summary: A group of investors including Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is set to secure European Union approval for its $55 billion acquisition …
a260717p10.title: Sources: the EU is set to approve the $55B acquisition of EA under its subsidy rules on July 30; the deal includes Saudi Arabia's PIF, Silver Lake, and Affinity (Foo Yun Chee/Reuters)

a260717p11.summary: General Compute, an AI inference cloud startup, has landed a $400 million loan from Upper90, a tech investment firm.
a260717p11.title: AI inference startup General Compute gets a $400M loan from tech investment firm Upper90, seemingly the first deal using inference-specific chips as collateral (Tim Fernholz/TechCrunch)

a260717p12.summary: ASML Holding NV will give employees globally a one-time €20,000 ($22,862) bonus, joining other firms in the chip industry …
a260717p12.title: ASML plans to give its ~45K employees globally a €20K bonus, issued as a share award that vests in 2030, joining other chip industry companies offering payouts (Sarah Jacob/Bloomberg)

a260717p13.summary: Transformer Weekly: NY data center moratorium, NDAA export controls and Amodei's $1m to safety super PAC
a260717p13.title: Kimi K3 hype shouldn't alarm the US about "losing the AI race" to China; K3 is a good model but not frontier-level and likely lacks dangerous cyber capabilities (Transformer)

a260717p14.summary: Kimi K3 may be an important inflection point for AI. Potentially negative for Anthropic and OpenAI while being net positive for essentially every other company in the world. I mean that very literally. Although the real “Sputnik moment” would be an open-source frontier model that [image]
a260717p14.title: Models like Kimi K3, Grok 4.5, and Muse 1.1 may prevent the dominance of 2-3 frontier labs with 90% inference margins from hurting other AI ecosystem layers (Gavin Baker/@gavinsbaker)

a260717p15.summary: A deal would underline how scarce computing power is for artificial intelligence development, and could create a new business for Meta.
a260717p15.title: Sources: Meta is in talks to rent computing power from its data centers to Anthropic in a deal that could be worth ~$10B over two years (New York Times)

a260717p16.summary: Apple Inc. and the US Justice Department are in early discussions about settling a 2024 lawsuit that alleges the iPhone maker violated antitrust laws.
a260717p16.title: Sources: Apple and the DOJ are in early discussions about settling a 2024 antitrust lawsuit alleging that Apple violated antitrust laws (Bloomberg)

a260717p17.summary: Apple has raised the price of Apple Music today in the United States and other countries. Starting today, Apple Music's Individual plan …
a260717p17.title: Apple raises the price of Apple Music, with the individual plan up by $1 to $11.99, and some Apple One bundles, the first price hike for Apple Music since 2022 (Chance Miller/9to5Mac)

a260717p18.summary: Elon Musk's company would sell military billions of dollars' worth of data-center capacity for running AI models
a260717p18.title: Sources: SpaceX is in talks with the DOD about providing the agency with access to data-center capacity worth billions of dollars for running AI models (Wall Street Journal)

a260717p19.summary: OpenRouter, which helps app developers access hundreds of AI models, has discussed a potential sale to a bigger tech company …
a260717p19.title: Sources: OpenRouter has discussed a potential sale to a bigger tech company that could value it at billions of dollars, a premium to its $1.3B valuation in May (The Information)

a260717p2.summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told employees Wednesday that Anthropic's limits on requests that users submit …
a260717p2.title: During an internal meeting, Satya Nadella criticized Claude Fable 5 for being "editorially controlled", saying its refusal to do "random things" makes no sense (Jordan Novet/CNBC)

a260717p20.summary: One UK man whose bill is usually less than £1 says he ‘almost had a heart attack’ when he saw £5.8bn invoice
a260717p20.title: Amazon apologizes after some AWS users received bills as high as $1.5T due to "an issue with unit pricing within the estimated billing computation subsystem" (Robert Booth/The Guardian)

a260717p21.summary: After raising prices on all Macs and iPads worldwide last month, Apple has now increased the price of iPhone models in Japan.
a260717p21.title: Apple raises iPhone prices in Japan by up to 11%, likely due to the Japanese yen's depreciation against the US dollar over the past year (Joe Rossignol/MacRumors)

a260717p22.summary: Valar Atomics, a three-year-old startup that makes small nuclear reactors intended to power data centers and other industrial facilities …
a260717p22.title: Sources: Valar Atomics, which makes small nuclear reactors intended to power data centers, is in talks to raise $1B at a ~$5B pre-money valuation (The Information)

a260717p23.summary: A brutal week for chip stocks — the same names that fueled this year's blistering market rally — has left investors from Seoul …
a260717p23.title: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index sank ~10% for the week, its largest weekly fall in over a year, and is now down ~20% from its late-June all-time high (Reuters)

a260717p24.summary: A group of major music publishers including Universal Music Group (UMG.AS) and Sony Music (6758.T) have agreed to end a legal dispute …
a260717p24.title: Filings: a group of major music publishers has agreed to end a copyright infringement dispute with X over the use of their music on the social media platform (Blake Brittain/Reuters)

a260717p25.summary: The Trump administration is considering plans for an independent regulator to vet the safety of artificial intelligence models with industry input …
a260717p25.title: Sources: the Trump administration is considering plans for an independent regulator to vet the safety of AI models; the regulator would report to the SEC (Bloomberg)

a260717p26.summary: Donald Trump's social media company has discussed charging Wall Street traders and investment firms as much as $100,000 a month …
a260717p26.title: Sources: Trump Media pitched a monthly fee of as much as $100K for the Truth API, with fast access to Trump's posts, for banks, algorithmic traders, and others (Reuters)

a260717p27.summary: Startup is also raising capital at $10 billion valuation in separate round led by Sequoia — Etched, a startup developing AI chips …
a260717p27.title: Sources: AI inference chip startup Etched is raising funds at a ~$20B valuation and is raising capital at a $10B valuation in a separate round led by Sequoia (Wall Street Journal)

a260717p28.summary: The U.S. Justice Department said on Friday that federal employees can now download the short-video app TikTok on government devices.
a260717p28.title: Memo: the DOJ says federal employees can now download TikTok on government devices, citing ByteDance's transfer of control of US operations to TikTok USDS (David Shepardson/Reuters)

a260717p29.summary: Hangzhou-based firm says users can control robots using only their thoughts via EEG headsets, marking a leap in embodied AI tech
a260717p29.title: China's BrainCo unveils what it says is the world's first integrated "brain-to-robot" platform that lets users control robots using an EEG headset (Minxiao Chang/South China Morning Post)

a260717p3.summary: Tata Electronics Pvt. is preparing to make India's first semiconductor wafers on far older technology than it originally expected …
a260717p3.title: Sources: Tata plans India's first large-scale chip fab in Dholera, Gujarat, mostly using 90nm nodes, a far humbler start than the 28nm node it touted earlier (Bloomberg)

a260717p30.summary: Beginning July 20, Claude Fable 5 will be included in all Max and Team Premium plans, at 50% of limits. Pro and Team Standard users will continue to have access to Fable via usage credits, and will receive a one-time $100 credit. Demand for Fable has been challenging to
a260717p30.title: Anthropic says Claude Fable 5 will be included in Max and Team Premium plans at 50% of limits from July 20 and via usage credits for Pro and Team Standard users (Claude/@claudeai)

a260717p4.summary: The social media giant's ambitions in developing data centers and computing resources are growing
a260717p4.title: Sources: Dave Brown, the outgoing SVP of AWS Compute, AI, and Platform, will join Meta in the coming weeks, where he will work on Meta's data center build-out (Anissa Gardizy/Wall Street Journal)

a260717p5.summary: Z.AI is on track for annual recurring revenue of $1 billion, a significant ramp-up for an enterprise-focused AI startup trying …
a260717p5.title: Sources: Z.ai is on track to achieve an annual recurring revenue of $1B, a first for a Chinese AI company, after achieving its full-year sales target in July (Bloomberg)

a260717p6.summary: The petition to Sundar Pichai, the CEO, included more than 4,500 signatures and included calls for buyout options
a260717p6.title: The Alphabet Workers Union sends a layoff protections petition with 4,500+ signatures to Sundar Pichai, calling for guaranteed severance, buyouts, and more (Sanya Mansoor/The Guardian)

a260717p7.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Friday cast Beijing as the champion of a new global AI order, using China's premier tech conference …
a260717p7.title: At the World AI Conference, Xi Jinping touts open-source AI, pledges to help the Global South build AI capabilities, and calls unequal AI access an "injustice" (Reuters)

a260717p8.summary: iPhone maker steps up aggressive tactics in trade secrets dispute with AI lab. Apple has targeted dozens of OpenAI employees …
a260717p8.title: Sources: Apple has sent personal legal warnings to ~40 former employees who now work at OpenAI, directing them to preserve documents and meet with its lawyers (Michael Acton/Financial Times)

a260717p9.summary: The City Attorney's Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 …
a260717p9.title: San Francisco sends legal notices to Apple and Google, demanding they take down 13 AI apps used to make deepfake nude images; Google says it deleted five apps (Matt Burgess/Wired)
